published_prior_art.cite: generic sharp-identified-set construction; Gunsilius2023DiSCo is a weighted quantile construction, not this compatibility-set statement

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_synth_control_factor_misspec_v1 is scalar Chebyshev partial ID; panel_sdid_mc_id_v1 is mean estimator span/equality

published_prior_art.cite: Gunsilius2023DiSCo gives DiSCo uniqueness/distance-preserving identification, not a rank-invariant finite-donor affine row-span iff boundary

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_synth_control_factor_misspec_v1 is scalar factor-misspecification bounds, not distributional rank-factor open-set nonidentification

published_prior_art.cite: Chen2020DistributionalSC and FermanPinto2021ImperfectFit concern poor/imperfect fit, not exact-pre-fit affine escape for full quantile curves
Gunsilius2023DiSCo + Bai2009IFE cover the stable convex/mean-factor ingredients, but not this exact loading-invariance corollary as stated

soundness_flags.label: Sanity-check corollary: Reduction to ordinary mean synthetic control
soundness_flags.one_line: The condition Q^0_it(u)=alpha_i+lambda_t is rank-constant and generally degenerate; the intended mean-SC reduction needs a common quantile shape such as H(u)+alpha_i+lambda_t plus integrability before integrating gives the usual mean identity.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Patch the mean-SC sanity check to use a common monotone base quantile H(u)+alpha_i+lambda_t and state the finite first-moment condition needed to integrate quantile identities.

Make the rank-factor convention explicit: either b_i is fixed across ranks, or write b_i(u) and define B(P,u) accordingly; this will prevent the row-span boundary from being read as ignoring cross-rank loading restrictions.
Add one sentence contrasting the proposal with recent/asymptotic DiSCo extensions, noting that those are estimation/inference papers and do not subsume the finite-donor identification boundary.

recommended_next_step: Revise the mean-SC sanity corollary and clarify the cross-rank loading convention; the flagship novelty kernel otherwise survives both repo and published checks.
